Bug / Task Description: Map.Set corrupts the sorted map when inserting a new key whose position is not at the very end. After growing the backing slices, the step that should shift the existing elements (from the insertion index onward) one position to the right to open a gap is not performed correctly, so the element previously occupying the insertion index is overwritten/lost and the key ordering and length become inconsistent. Get on a previously inserted key can then fail. Fix Set so that inserting a new key shifts the existing entries right and preserves all entries in sorted key order, while still overwriting in place when the key already exists.
Commit Subject: fix(treemap): shift existing entries right when inserting a new key
